rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe but uncomplicated atopic dermatitis flare — needs prompt prescription and self-care with a clear safety-net for eczema herpeticum and bacterial superinfection; not an emergency now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Clusters of small punched-out identical sores, rapidly worsening painful rash, or fever/feeling very unwell (eczema herpeticum)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek urgent/emergency assessment (ED) the same day
escalation_edges.rationale: Eczema herpeticum needs urgent systemic aciclovir and can disseminate.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get clusters of small, identical punched-out sores, the rash rapidly worsens and becomes very painful, or you get a fever — go to the emergency department the same day.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Honey-coloured crusting, hot swollen spreading skin, or fever (bacterial superinfection)
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the skin becomes hot, swollen and crusted with golden-yellow crusts, or you get a fever — get urgent review for antibiotics.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can help apply treatments and watch for the emergency features.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Use lots of moisturiser plus the steroid ointment on the red areas, keep showers lukewarm, humidify the air, and use the night antihistamine to sleep. Watch for punched-out sores with fever, or hot honey-crusted skin.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription/review; ED same-day for herpetic features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; ED for punched-out sores + fever or spreading hot infection.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a severe atopic dermatitis flare (herpeticum/bacterial screened negative), treatment started, and plan for maintenance therapy.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Self-care only with no eczema-herpeticum/bacterial safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving eczema herpeticum is missed and disseminates before the patient seeks care.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling that leaves a severe flare under-treated and itch/sleep unaddressed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency escalation for an uncomplicated eczema flare without herpetic/bacterial features.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot closely inspect for early punched-out erosions or subtle honey crusting remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the flare with fundamentals and give an explicit eczema-herpeticum/bacterial safety-net with a low threshold to be reviewed.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Steroids alone without a herpeticum safety-net, or dismissing the flare.
